headline: 250 years of American independence: Thomas Jefferson and John Adams, 'the North and South poles of the American Revolution'
description: 'America 250' (4/13). Fifty years to the day after the adoption of the Declaration of Independence, two of its architects died: the enigmatic Thomas Jefferson and the impulsive John Adams. This coincidence only strengthened, among the population, the sense of an unprecedented destiny promised to their nation.

headline: 'I almost faint every time I stand up': In cramped student housing, studying becomes impossible during the heatwave

How cities in Africa are adapting to intense and deadly heat
description: Reflective paints, green roofs, work breaks: Many African countries are seeking innovative ways to cope with increasingly extreme temperatures.

headline: Iran intensifies internal crackdown as US and Israel wage war on regime
description: While diplomatic talks between Tehran and Washington focus on Iran's nuclear program and sanctions, NGOs are warning that executions and mass arrests continue.

headline: Sam Barlow: The art of fragmented storytelling from the creator of 'Her Story' and 'Immortality'
description: Since 'Her Story' (2015), the video game designer has subverted traditional narrative mechanics, immersing players in a profusion of video fragments and leaving them to piece the story back together.

headline: French tolerance remains strong, but racism and antisemitism still run deep, report finds
description: In its annual report released on Thursday, the National Consultative Commission on Human Rights expresses concern about the persistence of xenophobic prejudices in French society, particularly among the younger generation.

headline: Heatwave in France spells uncertainty for several EDF nuclear reactors, but RTE says the overall grid is secure
description: France's state-owned electric utility company has shut down one nuclear reactor and reduced the output of two others. Although the extreme heat is increasing electricity demand, France has enough capacity to meet it, according to the national power grid operator.

headline: France's public pools need urgent renovation: 'What would we do without them? We wouldn't make it'
description: Aging, energy-intensive and expensive for rural towns to maintain, many aquatic centers in the countryside have been shutting down. Yet these facilities provide equal access for people to cool off and learn to swim. An MP recently sounded the alarm about the worrying situation facing these essential local public services.
